items.headline: Raptors fail test against Kings as DeRozan, Achiuwa burn former team
items.blurb: Raptors fail test against Kings as DeRozan, Achiuwa burn former team — A home game against the Kings was supposed to be an open-book test. The Raptors flunked it. And it was two former Raptors who played starring roles for the Kings in a game Toronto needed to win.

items.headline: Celtics' Jayson Tatum shuts down injury worries after triple-double vs. Heat
items.blurb: Celtics' Jayson Tatum shuts down injury worries after triple-double vs. Heat — For those who may have been wondering whether Boston Celtics forward Jayson Tatum rushed his return from a right Achilles tear, don't be.

items.headline: Wembanyama has 41 points, 18 rebounds as Spurs beat Warriors for 10th straight win
items.blurb: Wembanyama has 41 points, 18 rebounds as Spurs beat Warriors for 10th straight win

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Spurs 127, Warriors 113
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Spurs 127, Warriors 113 — Victor Wembanyama registered 41 points and 18 rebounds for his second consecutive game over 40 points and Stephon Castle added 15 points and 11 assists in the San Antonio Spurs’ 127-113 victory over the Golden State Warriors.

items.headline: Knicks' Karl-Anthony Towns takes game to new level in win over Grizzlies
items.blurb: Knicks' Karl-Anthony Towns takes game to new level in win over Grizzlies — To get the New York Knicks back on track, Karl-Anthony Towns had to take his game to a level it had yet to reach this season.

items.headline: NBA Hall of Famer Robert Parish sends clear message to legends criticizing today's stars
items.blurb: NBA Hall of Famer Robert Parish sends clear message to legends criticizing today's stars — A lot of former players have issues with how the game has changed, and it usually circles back to physicality.

items.headline: Tampering violations make Trail Blazers' selection of Yang Hansen more baffling
items.blurb: Tampering violations make Trail Blazers' selection of Yang Hansen more baffling — No team would break NBA rules to add rookie center Yang Hansen, except for the Portland Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Lack of urgency and consistent effort sees Raptors fall short vs. Kings
items.blurb: Lack of urgency and consistent effort sees Raptors fall short vs. Kings — Eric Smith and Michael Grange break down the Toronto Raptors’ disappointing home loss to the Sacramento Kings and discuss what the team needs to do to bounce back ahead of their upcoming trip to Memphis.

items.headline: Defense takes night off Wednesday in NBA
items.blurb: Defense takes night off Wednesday in NBA — The NBA has 12 days remaining in the regular season. On Wednesday, a number of defenses took the night off early.

items.headline: Report: Rapper J. Cole to lace up in Chinese Basketball Association
items.blurb: Report: Rapper J. Cole to lace up in Chinese Basketball Association — Cole, who last suited up for the Scarborough Shooting Stars of the Canadian Elite Basketball League, will take his talents overseas after signing a contract to play with the Nanjing Monkey Kings of the Chinese Basketball Association, ESPN’s Shams Charania reported Wednesday.

items.headline: The 'Active 2K-point NBA seasons' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Active 2K-point NBA seasons' quiz — How many of the 21 active NBA players to score at least 2,000 points in a single season can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 130, Jazz 117
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 130, Jazz 117 — Canada’s Jamal Murray went off for 35 points, and Nikola Jokic posted a triple-double with 15 points, 17 rebounds, and 12 assists as the Denver Nuggets defeated the Utah Jazz 130-117.

items.headline: Raptors’ Barrett sounds off after loss to Kings: ‘A game we’re supposed to win’
items.blurb: Raptors’ Barrett sounds off after loss to Kings: ‘A game we’re supposed to win’ — Toronto Raptors forward RJ Barrett discusses the team’s loss to the Sacramento Kings, saying bluntly that this is a game the team is “supposed to win no matter what” and that they can’t lose a game like that.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Kings 123, Raptors 115
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Kings 123, Raptors 115 — DeMar DeRozan made all twelve of his free-throw attempts in the game and scored 26 of his 28 points in the second half while Precious Achiuwa scored 28 and grabbed 19 rebounds to lead the Sacramento Kings to a 123-115 win over the Toronto Raptors.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Knicks 130, Grizzlies 119
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Knicks 130, Grizzlies 119 — OG Anunoby posted a double-double with 25 points and 13 rebounds, and Karl-Anthony Towns supplied 20 points and 11 rebounds as the New York Knicks defeated the Memphis Grizzlies 130-119.

items.headline: NBA Fines Blazers $100,000, Suspends Two Executives For Illegal Contact With Yang Hansen
items.blurb: NBA Fines Blazers $100,000, Suspends Two Executives For Illegal Contact With Yang Hansen — The NBA fined the Portland Trail Blazers $100,000 on Wednesday and suspended two team executives for improper contact made with center Yang Hansen in December 2023, nearly two years before the 7-foot Chinese big man was eligible for the draft. items.headline: NBA Highlights: Rockets 119, Bucks 113
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Rockets 119, Bucks 113 — Alperen Sengun scored 25 points and grabbed eight rebounds to lead the Houston Rockets to a 119-113 win over the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Bill Self Commits To Kansas For 2026-27 Season After Health Concerns
items.blurb: Bill Self Commits To Kansas For 2026-27 Season After Health Concerns — Kansas head coach Bill Self announced Wednesday he will return to coach the Jayhawks next season, ending weeks of uncertainty surrounding his future following a series of health concerns. Self said the decision came "with renewed clarity and the ongoing support from our administration." "I remain focused and committed to Kansas

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Pacers 145, Bulls 126
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pacers 145, Bulls 126 — Pascal Siakam led the way with 25 points, while Ethan Thompson posted a career-high 24 points with five assists, as the Indiana Pacers defeated the Chicago Bulls 145-126.

items.headline: Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu Both Announce Plans To Re-Sign With Liberty
items.blurb: Breanna Stewart, Sabrina Ionescu Both Announce Plans To Re-Sign With Liberty — Two-time WNBA MVP Breanna Stewart announced she will remain with the New York Liberty in free agency, keeping the league's most decorated roster largely intact heading into the 2026 season. Teammate Sabrina Ionescu separately confirmed her intention to re-sign with New York on Wednesday at USA Basketball training camp.

items.headline: Hawks rout Magic for 17th win in 19 games
items.blurb: Hawks rout Magic for 17th win in 19 games

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Celtics 147, Heat 129
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Celtics 147, Heat 129 — Jaylen Brown poured in 43 points on 17-of-29 shooting and Jayson Tatum tallied 25 points, 18 rebounds, and 11 assists to lead the Boston Celtics to a 147-129 victory over the Miami Heat.

items.headline: DeRozan drains three to pass Dominique Wilkins on NBA all-time scoring list
items.blurb: DeRozan drains three to pass Dominique Wilkins on NBA all-time scoring list — Watch as DeMar DeRozan knocks down a three-pointer in the third quarter against the Toronto Raptors, passing Dominique Wilkins for 17th all-time in NBA scoring.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Hawks 130, Magic 101
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Hawks 130, Magic 101 — Nickeil Alexander-Walker made five three-pointers and led all scorers with 32 points while Jalen Johnson tallied 18 points, 14 rebounds, and eight assists to lead the Atlanta Hawks to a 130-101 victory over the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Raptors’ defence turns over Kings, leading to pair of rack attacks
items.blurb: Raptors’ defence turns over Kings, leading to pair of rack attacks — Toronto Raptors forward RJ Barrett gets a feed from Ja’Kobe Walter and throws down a two-handed dunk, while on the ensuing play, the Raptors’ pressure leads to a turnover and Scottie Barnes dishes to Collin Murray-Boyles for the tomahawk jam.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: 76ers 153, Wizards 131
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: 76ers 153, Wizards 131 — Paul George scored 39 points and added six assists to lead the Philadelphia 76ers to a 153-131 win over the Washington Wizards.

items.headline: Grizzlies' Brandon Clarke arrested on reckless driving, drug charges
items.blurb: Grizzlies' Brandon Clarke arrested on reckless driving, drug charges — Brandon Clarke's season was already over before this week. Now, the Memphis Grizzlies forward is facing charges that go way beyond the court.

items.headline: Trail Blazers fined $100K for contacting Yang Hansen before draft eligibility
items.blurb: Trail Blazers fined $100K for contacting Yang Hansen before draft eligibility — The NBA announced Wednesday that the Trail Blazers have been fined $100,000 for violating league rules governing contact with draft-ineligible players after scouting Hansen in Dec. 2023.

items.headline: ‘We’re trying to get him back to playing soon’: Darko on Quickley’s injury
items.blurb: ‘We’re trying to get him back to playing soon’: Darko on Quickley’s injury — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajaković addresses Immanuel Quickley’s injury, saying the guard is currently working out in a boot as the team looks to get him back on the court soon.

items.headline: Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking
items.blurb: Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking — Try as the NBA might, but tanking isn't going away. While pondering how to de-incentivize losing, commissioner Adam Silver must consider the nuclear option.

items.blurb: Steph Curry sets target date for his return from injury — Steph Curry appears determined to try to come back for the Golden State Warriors before the end of the season.

items.headline: Did Luka Doncic commit a strategic technical foul?
items.blurb: Did Luka Doncic commit a strategic technical foul? — Luka Doncic spent weeks on the verge of a one-game suspension after receiving his 15th technical foul of the season. He finally drew that suspension for the Los Angeles Lakers' easiest remaining game, against the lowly Washington Wizards.

items.blurb: Tom Izzo Gave Serious Consideration To Taking Suns Head Coaching Job — Tom Izzo was offered the head coaching position of the Phoenix Suns last offseason. The job eventually went to Jordan Ott, who worked under Izzo for several years at Michigan State before eventually moving to the NBA.
